positions.name: iShares Core S&P 500 UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Core broad market exposure to S&P 500. IE-domiciled accumulating structure eliminates 30% WHT on distributions — structurally optimal vehicle for a Lebanese resident. Portfolio anchor targeting ~36% of NAV. APRIL 22 UPDATE: Position at 33.96% of NAV (1.8025 shares, $1,375.24 market value, +6.34% unrealized). Gap to 36% target: approximately $83 at current NAV. ASML exit proceeds ($150-170) to be deployed here immediately on ASML exit. Post-deployment target weight: ~36-37% of NAV. S&P 500 near all-time highs (~7,064 April 21 close). IT sector at 31.9% of S&P 500 weight, recovering strongly. Thesis conviction: STRONGER. No structural invalidation conditions triggered.
invalidation_conditions: Structural shift away from US equities as the dominant long-term growth engine
A superior WHT-efficient broad market vehicle becomes available

positions.name: Amazon.com Inc
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy entered during Liberation Day tariff shock. Core thesis: AWS cloud dominance (~33% global cloud market share), advertising revenue at double-digit growth, and AI infrastructure investment (Bedrock, Trainium custom silicon). APRIL 22 UPDATE: BofA and KeyBanc double-upgraded pre-earnings with $298 and $325 price targets respectively (TD Cowen at $300). AWS growth expected to accelerate from Q4's 24% to 28-30% in Q1 2026, driven by Anthropic-related revenues and custom chip business (Trainium/Graviton $10B+ annual revenue run rate). Q1 2026 earnings confirmed April 29, 2026 at 5:30 PM ET. Street consensus: $1.63 EPS, $177-178B revenue, $20.7B EBIT. 42 analyst Buys vs 3 Holds. $286 average price target (+15% upside from current $248.28). As of April 20: up +16.05% from entry. No pre-earnings adds — RSI elevated. Review ADD $50-75 if April 29 results show AWS >25% AND EBIT above $20B AND Q2 guidance maintained or raised.
invalidation_conditions: AWS loses meaningful cloud market share to Azure or GCP on a sustained basis
Regulatory breakup that separates AWS from the retail/advertising business
Sustained margin compression across all segments (retail, advertising, AND cloud simultaneously)
Q1 2026 operating income below the low end of the $16.5B guidance floor with a Q2 guide-down

positions.name: SPDR Gold Shares
positions.thesis: Macro hedge against dollar weakness, geopolitical risk, and inflationary pressure. Gold has no cash flows so carries zero WHT exposure despite US domicile. APRIL 22 UPDATE: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 18-19, citing US 'breaches of trust' after keeping naval blockade. Ceasefire expires April 23. Nuclear talks fully collapsed — Iran refuses to end uranium enrichment, dismantle facilities, or transfer HEU stockpile. GLD at $442.09 (April 20 close) — 5.3% above the $420 invalidation trigger. The 4-week sustained risk-on clock for GLD invalidation has NOT started and is fully reset. Institutional targets: Goldman Sachs end-2026 $5,400/oz; JPMorgan Q4 2026 average $5,055/oz; BNP Paribas year-end $6,000/oz. Central bank purchases: 755-850 tonnes forecast 2026, PBOC running 16 consecutive months of reserve building. Thesis conviction: STRONGER than prior report. Every pillar intact and strengthening.
invalidation_conditions: Sustained USD strengthening cycle driven by structurally higher real rates that persist above inflation
Major reversal in central bank gold buying trend (multiple major central banks becoming net sellers)
GLD breaks and holds below $420 — triggers 25% trim rebalance into CSPX

Iran war fully and durably resolved AND Strait of Hormuz formally reopened AND risk-on environment maintained for 4+ consecutive weeks — assess 25% trim at that point (4-week clock fully reset as of April 18-19 Hormuz re-closure; ceasefire expiry April 23 does NOT restart the clock without a formal peace deal)

positions.name: Mastercard Inc
positions.wht_exposure: 30% WHT on dividend — confirmed $0.22 withheld on $0.73 April dividend. Annualised WHT drag <$1 at current position size. Immaterial.
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy during Liberation Day volatility. Thesis: payments network duopoly (MA + Visa) with near-monopoly economics, asset-light model generating high ROE, global consumer spending volume exposure, and pricing power. APRIL 22 UPDATE: As of April 20, up +0.66% unrealized ($516.65 vs cost $513.24). 35/38 analysts Buy/Outperform, zero sells; $654.66 average price target (+26% upside from current). EARNINGS DATE CONFIRMED: Q1 2026 earnings report April 30, 2026. Q1 covers January-March (largely pre-peak Iran escalation), so volumes should be clean. Q1 2026 guidance: net revenue growth at low end of low-double digits on a currency-neutral basis (FX tailwind +3.5-4.0pp). Key watch metrics for ADD $100 decision on April 30: cross-border volume must exceed Q4 2025's 14% rate AND VAS growth must sustain above 20% (Q4 exit rate 22%) AND guidance maintained or raised. Stagflation risk from Iran/Hormuz is a medium-term headwind to cross-border volumes but unlikely to show in Q1 data. Dividend yield <1% means WHT drag is under $1/year at current size.
invalidation_conditions: Disintermediation by real-time payment rails (FedNow, open banking) gaining scale at the expense of card networks
Regulatory caps on interchange fees in core US or European markets
Q1 2026 earnings miss on cross-border volumes or management guides down — re-evaluate immediately on April 30
Sustained global consumer spending contraction lasting multiple quarters

positions.name: NVIDIA Corporation
positions.thesis: AI infrastructure buildout driving unprecedented GPU demand. NVDA holds dominant position in AI training and inference hardware; CUDA ecosystem creates deep switching costs. APRIL 22 UPDATE: As of April 20, up +11.04% from entry ($202.06 vs cost $181.98). CRITICAL NEW DATA: Vera Rubin platform confirmed in full production, on track for H2 2026 (Bank of America research note). Vera Rubin delivers 90% AI inference cost reduction and 75% fewer GPUs for training vs Blackwell — represents the next structural re-rating catalyst. Combined order backlog for Blackwell AND Vera Rubin through 2027 DOUBLED to $1 trillion (up from prior $500B guidance from Jensen Huang). Blackwell systems sold out through mid-2026. Hyperscalers collectively committing $600B+ in 2026 AI capex. 38 analysts Strong Buy; $273 consensus price target (+35% upside), Evercore ISI at $380 high. Q1 FY2027 earnings approximately May 20, 2026. Do not add at elevated $202 levels; reassess on $180-185 pullback. Thesis conviction: STRONGEST IN PORTFOLIO.
invalidation_conditions: AMD or custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Microsoft Maia) takes meaningful and sustained share in AI training workloads
AI capex cycle reverses — hyperscalers visibly cut or guide down infrastructure spend across multiple quarters
Export restrictions to China expand materially beyond current guidance, reducing total addressable market by >15%
Post-GTC 2026: $1T order backlog proves overstated or a significant portion of orders cancelled in subsequent quarters
Vera Rubin production delays extending beyond Q3 2026 — would defer the next re-rating catalyst

positions.name: ASML Holding NV
positions.wht_exposure: 15% Dutch WHT assumed (treaty rate). Verification still outstanding. NOW MOOT — FULL EXIT RECOMMENDED AND MAINTAINED.
positions.thesis: THESIS FLAG: DETERIORATING — FULL EXIT RECOMMENDED. MAINTAINED AS OF 2026-04-22. EUV lithography monopoly remains structurally intact (no credible competitor on a 5-10 year horizon). However, ALL material invalidation conditions are simultaneously materializing: (1) China mix collapsed from 36% (Q4 2025) to 19% (Q1 2026) in a single quarter — on a clear trajectory to breach the 15% invalidation threshold in Q2 2026 results (approximately July 2026). (2) A bipartisan group of US lawmakers has proposed a bill specifically cutting off ASML's sale of DUV machines to Chinese chip companies — JPMorgan warns this could cut EPS by up to 10%. (3) Stock fell 6% post-Q1 results despite a beat (EUR 8.8B revenue vs EUR 8.5B consensus, EPS EUR 7.15 vs EUR 6.00) and a full-year guidance raise to EUR 36-40B — the market is actively pre-pricing the multiple de-rating. As of April 20: +5.68% unrealized ($1,476.50 vs cost $1,397.14). FULL EXIT REQUIRED this week (April 22-25). Redeploy $150-170 into CSPX immediately post-exit; reserve $100 for MA April 30 conditional add; retain $50 buffer.
invalidation_conditions: China develops a credible domestic EUV alternative — assessed as a 5-10 year horizon risk, not near-term
Semiconductor capex supercycle ends and fab customers cancel or significantly delay EUV orders at scale
Dutch or US government export restrictions expand materially beyond current ASML guidance, causing revenue guidance to be CUT (not merely compressed China mix)
China mix falls below 15% of net sales in Q2 2026 results — APPROACHING THRESHOLD: currently at 19% and declining rapidly (was 36% in Q4 2025)
US DUV restriction bill passes into law — bipartisan bill NOW ACTIVELY ADVANCING in Congress as of April 2026
Additional China export restriction threat beyond existing controls — CONFIRMED materializing April 20, 2026

positions.name: iShares USD Treasury Bond 0-3 Month UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Cash equivalent / capital preservation layer with 0% effective WHT. Replaced SGOV (which carried 30% WHT on distributions) — structurally optimal for Lebanese residents. US Treasury interest paid to an Irish-domiciled fund qualifies for the portfolio interest exemption from NRA withholding. Near-zero duration risk (0-3M). APRIL 22 UPDATE: Position at 14.83% of total portfolio NAV ($600.61, -1.22% unrealized). YTM approximately 3.6% embedded in NAV — above the 3.0% trim trigger but below the ~4.3% thesis baseline. Fed holding at 3.50-3.75%; FOMC April 28-29 at 99.4% no-change probability. JPMorgan forecasts no Fed cuts in 2026 (possible 25bps hike Q3 2027). Iran re-closure of Hormuz on April 18-19 maintains near-term capital preservation rationale. STAGE-DOWN PLAN ACTIVE: Target reduction from ~14.83% toward ~10% of NAV in stages. April 28-29 FOMC is the next trigger: dovish signal = first stage-down of ~$100 redeployed into CSPX. Do not trim before FOMC clarity.
invalidation_conditions: Fed cuts rates aggressively (>100bps in 2026) — opportunity cost vs equities rises significantly, accelerate trim
A better WHT-efficient fixed income vehicle with superior risk-adjusted return is identified
Portfolio NAV grows large enough (>$10K) that maintaining a 15% cash equivalent layer imposes unacceptable drag on long-term returns
YTM falls below 3.0% — at that point, reduce toward 8-10% of NAV immediately
